title: Cirrus: Install Go 1.11 on Ubuntu VMs
body:
There is no native package for this, so the packaged version must also
be installed, otherwise all the support/dependencies would be removed
also (like go-md2man).  Fix this by installing from the google released
tarball, into /usr/local/go and set $GOROOT to point there.

Also, include a small fix for hack/get_ci_vm.sh not installing
testing dependencies because of an old assumption.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: When it passes we can merge.

Interesting to see the across the board time-increase.  +20min almost.  Even tested bumping memory up to 8gig w/o any effect.  The increase in runtime appears to have come in between: [PR 2387](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6271059205554176) and [PR 2394](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5197794982756352).
Bot never added a size label, so I'm wondering if it's working
